notes: SHIP v1.14.350 - LOADER ARITY SPLIT: the .347/.348 bug class is now UNREPRESENTABLE, not merely visible. Owner approved after the .349 report. THE MECHANISM BEING KILLED: each deployment loader changed SHAPE based on arity - deploy_loadRacks(deployId) returned ONE deployment's racks, deploy_loadRacks() returned EVERY deployment's, and nothing at the call site said which you had. Hand the scoped result to a whole-key writer and you silently delete every other deployment's records: that is exactly what .348 was. .348 fixed the one offending call site and .349 renamed the savers so the mismatch is visible - but a visible trap is still a trap. CHANGE: each of the four loaders is split into two named doors with NO arity branch. deploy_loadRacks -> deploy_loadRacksFor(deployId) + deploy_loadAllRacks(); same for Phases, Optics, Audit. You now have to NAME which set you want, and a scoped read can no longer silently become a whole-store read. 86 scoped call sites + 4 unscoped, all converted; zero residue of the four old identifiers in code. THE NON-CALL REFERENCES MATTERED MOST: 11 'typeof deploy_loadX === function' guards (8 Racks, 3 Optics; Phases and Audit never had any) plus 3 comments. A rename that only touched call sites would have left those guards evaluating FALSE - the guarded features (Command rack counts, the OPEN BAY rack source, active-context optics, reconcile ledger) would have gone quietly dead with NO error at all, which is the No-Silent-Failures rule failing by omission. All renamed and each verified to name a live function. ONE DELIBERATE BEHAVIOR CHANGE, called out rather than hidden inside a rename: a falsy deployId used to fall through and return the ENTIRE store - the silent failure that made .348 possible. deploy_loadXFor(falsy) now reports and returns []. Audited all 86 scoped call sites by argument expression, and rd-review independently traced every user-facing entry point (including deep links, QR rack restore, back-nav restore and setTimeout-deferred calls) to a deploy_getById + phantomToast guard that fires BEFORE any loader runs - so this branch is UNREACHABLE through the app today. It is a tripwire for future call sites, not a live behavior change. The report goes through phantom_logErr via one shared _deploy_loaderMisuse helper, NOT console.warn (rd-review advisory, adopted): it lands in the on-device crash log, so a stray call site surfaces on the phone in the aisle instead of only in a laptop's devtools - a tripwire only a developer can see is half a tripwire. THE 4 CONVERTED UNSCOPED SITES: exactly TWO - checklist_toggle, checklist_setNote - mutate a phase found by globally-unique phaseId inside the unscoped array and round-trip through deploy_saveAllPhases, so their read and write scopes match by construction and a scoped read there would reproduce the .348 clobber for Phases. The other TWO only READ the unscoped array to locate a phase by id and never write DEPLOY_PHASES_KEY: checklist_addItem sets the transient _checklist_open render map and persists its own change to a DIFFERENT key (CHECKLIST_SITE_KEY), and ge_onCompleteTap's unchecked-items soft guard writes nothing at all; both could have used the scoped loader and stayed correct. Two successive drafts of this note over-counted the save-paired sites (first as four, then as three) - corrected to two in both the note and the source comment after rd-review traced each site's actual persist target. No runtime effect either way; the code was always correct, only the annotation was wrong. No CSS, no markup, no rd-gating -> ?legacy=1 byte-identical. Gates: inline-JS vm.Script x3, CSS brace net-0, three-stamp .349->.350, residue + call-count parity, exactly-one-definition for all 8 resulting functions with no collision against the unrelated pre-existing deploy_loadAll (DEPLOY_KEY, the deployment list), no bare-arg For() calls and no argumented All() calls. New .350 suite on the shipped split plus all prior suites re-run green (.346 25/25, .347 9/9, .348 10/10). phantom-ship-gate PASS, phantom-rd-reviewer PASS. DEVICE-VERIFY (clear SW first, regression sweep - no new feature): with TWO deployments, open each and confirm its racks/phases/optics/audit are ITS OWN and complete; advance a phase, block one with a note, tick a checklist item, dispense an optic, assign a rack; reopen both deployments and confirm nothing leaked between them and nothing vanished. ROLLBACK: revert this commit; it is independent of .348's data fix.
